world.title: colonial activist grabbing attention in West Africa: Who is Kemi Seba?
world.description: The controversial anti-colonial pan-African activist Kemi Seba is currently in detention in South Africa, days after his arrest while allegedly attempting to flee to Europe via Zimbabwe.

world.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
world.description: Donald Trump cancelled a planned trip by US officials to Pakistan for talks on the Iran war on Saturday, shortly after Tehran's delegation had left Islamabad.

world.title: Rebels blamed for Colombian highway bombing that killed 14
world.description: Political violence has escalated in Colombia ahead of next month's presidential election.

world.title: 40 years after Chernobyl: Pripyat today
world.description: After the catastrophic accident in the nearby nuclear reactor, the city of Pripyat had to be completely evacuated. Some 50,000 people left their homes forever. DW visited the town with a former resident 40 years later.

world.title: US-Iran peace hopes fade as Trump scraps talks
world.description: ISLAMABAD/WASHINGTON, April 26 - Hopes of a diplomatic breakthrough in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran receded as a new week began, with talks aimed at ending the two-month conflict at a standstill and both Tehran and Washington showing little willingness to soften their terms. Read more at straitstimes.com.

world.title: Colombia highway bombing leaves 14 dead amid ‘wave of attacks’
world.description: The attack tore apart a busy road, according to local authorities, as deadly violence resurges in the country.

world.title: Mexico City Spanish immersion bootcamp: Can a week make you fluent?
world.description: Can a week in a Spanish language bootcamp make you fluent? Lucy Pearson travels to Mexico City and discovers how communication changes the way you experience...

world.title: The slow erosion of Japan's arms export restraints
world.description: Tokyo’s expansion of defence exports is less a rupture with pacifism than the culmination of decades of incremental loosening

business.title: NUPRC urges refiners to acquire oil blocks
business.description: Nigeria's NUPRC advises refinery owners to acquire oil blocks, addressing long-term crude supply challenges and boosting domestic refining capacity.

business.title: "Made with Jeongeup Rehmannia"… himchanmaru Launches Premium Ssanghwa Tea
business.description: SEOUL, South Korea, April 26, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- himchanmaru has introduced a premium ssanghwa tea as part of its strategy to expand into the traditional herbal beverage market.

business.title: SK hynix receives 2026 IEEE Corporate Innovation Award for Driving AI Computing Expansion with HBM

business.title: Pizza By Drone: Little Caesars And Flytrex Make History
business.description: Dinner's served by drone in the new Flytrex Sky2, even if it's two large pizzas and two large drinks. And it comes in just under five minutes.

business.title: We thought Gen Z weren't drinking alcohol. But do canned cocktails suggest otherwise?
business.description: Gen Z is starting their nights with sugary canned cocktails - even if the taste proves divisive.

business.title: How the AI-driven data center boom is leading to skyrocketing energy bills for many Americans
business.description: A CBS News analysis found that Georgia Power, the largest energy provider in the state, imposed six rate hikes in the last three years.

business.title: Energy sovereignty: remote Indigenous towns are investing in microgrids to power their homes
business.description: Most communities rely on government-supplied diesel generators. Solar microgrids offer a secure supply – and residents can own the infrastructure

business.title: How Simran Kaur hit her FIRE number at 29 and still chooses to work
business.description: Simran Kaur retired early. Like really, really early – at 29. In just 10 years she has achieved what many don’t reach in 40 – having enough invested to cover...

science.title: Bonuses can lower self-set goals and reduce performance, experiment suggests
science.description: Financial bonuses are often used to motivate employees to meet targets and boost productivity. But do they actually work? New research from Tilburg University suggests these incentives can sometimes have the opposite effect. Employees who set their own goals often perform better without a financial bonus.

science.title: Black hole GW190521 may be a wormhole from another universe
science.description: A new study tests whether GW190521 was a wormhole echo, but the black hole merger explanation still fits the data better.

science.title: Giant exoplanet shows unexpected atmospheric signals
science.description: Cirrus-like clouds have been discovered on a giant planet 12 light-years away, a finding that could change the search for Earth-like worlds.

science.title: Scientists Shocked to Discover Dinosaur Footprints in South Africa
science.description: Scientists have discovered dinosaur footprints along South Africa’s coastline, offering new evidence that dinosaurs continued to live in southern Africa

science.title: Scientists Unearth Impossible Fossils Trapped In Rust Beneath Australian Farmland
science.description: A new discovery in Australia reveals fossils preserved in iron with astonishing detail, reshaping how scientists search for ancient life.

science.title: Scientists Say a Hidden Structure May Exist Inside Earth’s Core
science.description: Deep within Earth’s core, scientists are uncovering signs of a hidden structure that challenges long-standing models of the planet’s interior.

science.title: You Should Never Touch A Meteorite With Your Bare Hands - Here's Why
science.description: The reason you don't want to touch a meteorite with your bare hands is because you could contaminate it, robbing it of its scientific value.

science.title: Fluorescent probe lights up centrioles and cilia in living cells across species
science.description: Scientists at EPFL have developed CenSpark, a fluorescent probe that makes centrioles and cilia visible inside living cells, helping researchers study cell division, development, and immunity like never before.

ai.title: World IP Day 2026: PitchMark launches Ideas.Exchange to help creators safeguard and license ideas in the age of AI
ai.description: SINGAPORE, April 26, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- To mark World IP Day 2026, PitchMark® today launched Ideas.Exchange, a first‑of‑its‑kind platform designed to help creators assert intellectual property rights, license ideas, and formalise creative conversations in an increasingly AI‑driven economy.

ai.title: 'Will shield vulnerable sections from price pain, says Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman
ai.description: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman pledges government support for farmers facing rising costs. She encourages Indian businesses to invest and expand capacity. The minister also highlights the dual nature of artificial intelligence, acknowledging its disruptive potential. Discussions are ongoing regarding advanced AI models and cybersecurity. The strategic sale of IDBI Bank is confirmed to proceed.

ai.title: 4.5% yield & Rs 5,510 crore cash: Is this zero-debt tech giant India’s safest dividend fortress?
ai.description: A cash-rich tech player is quietly delivering strong returns and hefty dividends. But with rising AI investments and an aggressive payout, can this income story sustain—or is a trade-off ahead?

ai.title: Think Twice Before Asking ChatGPT These Personal Questions
ai.description: AI is becoming part of everyday life, with many relying on platforms like ChatGPT. A Cornell University study finds chatbots often over-validate users compared to humans.

ai.title: Dataland AI art museum to open in June in LA : NPR
ai.description: As some continue to pushback on AI art, artist Refik Anadol seeks to base his new venture on ethical and environmental principles.

ai.title: Techtonic: How to boost real intelligence with AI
ai.description: From JEE preparation to college work, students can enhance understanding with low-cost tools — just don’t let AI think for you.
